'''Tyla Hepi''' (born 15 June 1993) is a [[New Zealand people|New Zealand]] professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Doncaster RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].<ref name="Toulouse add Tyla Hepi">{{cite web|url=http://www.loverugbyleague.com/news_20705-toulouse-add-tyla-hepi.html|title=Toulouse add Tyla Hepi|publisher= loverugbyleague|date=2016-01-27|accessdate=2016-09-07}}</ref>

==Playing career==
He previously played for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] and [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Hull KR]] at the [[Gloucestershire All Golds]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]]. Hepi has also played for [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] and [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and the [[Wyong Roos]] in the [[NSW Cup]].<ref name="Hepi makes Player of the Year shortlist">{{cite web|url=https://allgoldsrugby.com/hepiplayeroftheyear/|title=Toulouse add Tyla Hepi|publisher=Gloucestershire All Golds|date=2013-12-03|accessdate=2016-09-07}}</ref> and in the [[Auckland Rugby League]] competition for the [[Point Chevalier Pirates]].<ref name=" Into his league">{{cite web|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/aucklander/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=1503376&objectid=11037830|title=Into his league|publisher=BBC|date=2011-08-11|accessdate=2016-09-07}}</ref>

===Doncaster RLFC===
On 24 Nov 2023 it was reported that he had signed for [[Doncaster RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=24 Nov 2023|title=Ambitious Championship club ‘beat Super League competition’ to secure signature of experienced forward who follows in father’s footsteps|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ambitious-championship-club-beat-super-league-competition-to-secure-signature-of-experienced-forward-who-follows-in-fathers-footsteps}}</ref>

==Personal==
Tyla is the son of coach and former player [[Brad Hepi]].<ref name=" Into his league"/>
